discovery_instructions: Identify what the main item/entity is for this feature (e.g., employee record, document, invitation)
Look for list/table views - the rows represent the primary entity
Check page headers and breadcrumbs for entity naming
Note the singular and plural forms used in the UI

find_trigger: Look for buttons with: + Add, + Create, + New, plus icon
Check top-right corner of the page
Look in empty state messages for create prompts
create.validation: New item appears in list with unique identifier

find_trigger: Click on any row in a list/table
Look for 'View' or eye icon buttons
Check if rows are clickable (cursor changes)
read.validation: Detail panel, modal, or page opens showing full information

find_trigger: Look for Edit button, pencil icon
Check for inline editing (click on field to edit)
Look for status change dropdowns or buttons
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for edit option
update.validation: Changes persist after save, reflected in list view

find_trigger: Look for Delete button, trash icon
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for delete option
Look for bulk delete with checkboxes
Some features use 'Archive' or 'Cancel' instead of delete
delete.validation: Item removed from list or status changed to deleted/archived

instructions: Navigate to the feature's main section using the navigation_map
Take a full page snapshot to understand the layout
Find ALL clickable elements: buttons, links, tabs, dropdowns, icons
Map the navigation structure: tabs, sidebar items, breadcrumbs
Click each tab/sub-section and document what appears
Locate CRUD controls for the primary entity
Check for action menus (three-dot menus, right-click menus)
Document any modals, dialogs, slide-out panels
Identify form fields and input types when forms appear
Look for filters, search, sort, and export options
Check for bulk action controls (checkboxes, select all)
Note any settings or configuration options
discovery_targets: List/table of items with row actions

nav_entries: Payroll > Air tickets > Employee air tickets
content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Both articles cover policy setup from different angles. Article 23328000633489 provides high-level job-to-be-done and hierarchical task structure; article 13937322643857 provides detailed step-by-step instructions and comprehensive rules/constraints. Consolidated into single admin workflow for policy creation.

nav_entries: Payroll > Air tickets > My air tickets
content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Single article covering employee-facing request submission via mobile app. Covers three redemption pathways: reimbursement, booking, and encashment.
Dedicated article on workflow automation for encashment requests. Covers workflow creation, trigger configuration, and integration with payroll system.
Grouped tickets describing inability to delete, reverse, or change state of air ticket requests after creation or processing. Includes post-payroll modifications and rejection reversals.
Grouped tickets describing missing policy configuration features: calendar year reset, carry-over rules, eligibility conditions, auto-encashment, and multi-policy assignment.
Grouped tickets describing RBAC restrictions preventing HR admins from self-service configuration and role management without super admin intervention.
Grouped tickets describing sequential approval requirements and missing notification mechanisms for managers outside standard approval flows.
Grouped tickets describing missing dedicated admin management section, audit trail visibility, and responsive UI design for air ticket administration.
Grouped tickets describing missing logic for employee offboarding and EOS integration with air ticket entitlements.
Grouped tickets describing missing dynamic cost processing and granular status transition controls for air ticket allowances.
Grouped tickets describing inconsistent request creation workflows and missing unified employee interface for managing requests.
Single ticket describing incomplete air ticket and travel expense tracking workflows within Work Expenses module.

Both tickets address inability to control who can submit Air Ticket requests and how approval workflows are configured. TSSD-3983 focuses on disabling employee submissions; TSSD-926 focuses on custom role assignment in approval chains.
Single ticket addressing inability to revert processed bookings to pending status, preventing corrections after submission.

detected_integrations.description: Employees submit air ticket requests through the Work section of the mobile app. Mobile app provides self-service interface for request submission and status tracking.

Air ticket encashment requests are created in Bayzat Payroll system. Auto encashment workflows target Bayzat Payroll as the application for request generation. Approved encashment requests integrate with payroll processing.
Encashment requests are managed and processed through the Finance menu. Finance module handles payroll deductions and allowance processing for air ticket encashments.
Air ticket requests follow configured approval flows in Automations > Approval flows. Auto encashment workflows can be configured to create fully approved or approval-pending requests.
Notifications are sent for air ticket request status changes and workflow execution events.
Air ticket policies are configured in Settings > Company > Air Tickets. Policy settings control allowance amounts, eligibility, redemption options, and request limits.
Air ticket entitlements and offboarding logic are not integrated. System cannot automatically handle benefit conversion or forfeiture when employees leave.
Air ticket entitlements are not automatically integrated with EOS calculations, requiring manual handling of benefit conversion.
Air ticket management is part of Work Expenses module but has incomplete workflows for submission, processing, and approval of travel expenses.
Air ticket policy configuration is restricted to super administrators only, preventing HR admins from self-service role creation and policy management.
Air Ticket request status transitions are rigid; processed bookings cannot be reverted, indicating tight coupling between booking confirmation and status immutability.

check_instructions: Navigate to Automations in the left sidebar
Click + Create workflow (or similar create button)
In trigger selection, search for this feature's name
Check if this feature appears as a trigger source
Document any trigger events found (e.g., 'item created', 'status changed')
Check Actions section for actions related to this feature
Look for 'Approval' or 'Pending Approval' status in the feature
Check if items have approval workflow indicators
Look for 'Submit for Approval' buttons or actions
Find approval settings in feature configuration
Document the approval chain (who approves what)
Test approval/rejection flow if test data available
Check for approval history or audit trail

what_to_do.task: Create and configure air ticket allowance policy
steps: Navigate to Settings > Company > Air Tickets
Click 'Add New' to create a new policy
Enter policy name and define allowance amount
Specify eligible individuals (by seniority, country, or other criteria)
Configure policy behavior: set request timing, define renewal cycle, and set maximum requests per cycle
Select redemption options: choose from reimbursement, encashment, and/or company booking
Select eligible employees to assign to this policy
Review policy details and finalize
Verify policy is active and ready for employee requests
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Air ticket policy is created and active. Employees assigned to the policy can now submit air ticket requests according to the configured allowance, renewal period, and redemption options. Policy settings are locked once requests are raised.
rules_and_constraints: Allowance amount can vary by employee seniority or country/region of residence
Allowance applies collectively to listed dependents, not individually
Each employee can be assigned to only one air ticket policy
Organizations can set maximum number of requests per renewal cycle
Multiple policies can be created for different hierarchy levels
Active policies with existing requests cannot be edited
Employee requests cannot exceed the allotted allowance amount
edge_cases: Policies can be tailored to different organizational needs and employee characteristics
Varying allowance based on employee seniority or country of residence requires separate policies
If policy has active requests, admin must create new policy for any changes rather than editing existing one

source_articles.title: Setting up an air ticket allowance policy

what_to_do.task: Submit air ticket request as employee
steps: Open Bayzat mobile app
Navigate to Work section
View existing air ticket requests and their status
Click 'Request Air Ticket' button
Choose request type from three options: Reimbursement, HR/Admin Booking, or Encashment
Provide required details for the selected request type
Submit request for approval
Track request status in the Work section
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Air ticket request is submitted successfully. Employee receives confirmation and can track request status through the mobile app. Request moves to approval workflow based on company configuration.
rules_and_constraints: All three air ticket request options (reimbursement, booking, encashment) must be enabled by admin before employees can use them
Encashment is only possible if the employee is not taking any leaves during the request period
Request must comply with the air ticket policy allowance amount and renewal cycle
edge_cases: Encashment requests are blocked if employee has active leave during the encashment period
If admin has not enabled specific redemption options, only enabled options appear to employee

what_to_do.task: Set up automatic air ticket encashment workflow
steps: Navigate to Settings > Workflows
Click to create a new workflow
Select 'Air Tickets Auto Encashment' recipe from available options
Define workflow trigger: set the date and time when encashment requests should be created
Define criteria: select which encashment options to include
Set request details: choose Bayzat Payroll as the application, select the encashment request type (fully approved or requiring approvals), and assign the employee
Save the workflow configuration
Verify workflow is activated and check workflow activity log for execution history
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Automated workflow is created and active. On the scheduled trigger date and time, encashment requests are automatically generated for assigned employees according to the configured criteria. Requests appear in the Finance menu and payroll system.
rules_and_constraints: Trigger time is based on account location (UAE or KSA timezone)
Time selection is limited to hour-level precision; minute selector is non-functional
Must select between fully approved requests or requests requiring approvals
Must assign at least one employee to the workflow
Workflow depends on predefined air ticket approval flows being configured
Encashment request type must be chosen from available options in the system
edge_cases: Time selection interface only supports hour-level granularity; minute field does not function
Workflow execution depends on existing air ticket approval workflow system being properly configured
If no employees are assigned, workflow will not generate any requests

source_articles.title: Air Tickets Auto Encashments via Workflows

what_to_do.task: View and manage employee air ticket requests
what_to_do.persona: HR Admin | Super Admin | People Manager
steps: Navigate to Payroll > Air tickets > Employee air tickets
View list of all employee air ticket requests
Filter or search for specific employee requests
Click on a request to view details (employee name, request type, allowance amount, status)
Review request status (pending, approved, rejected, processed)
Approve or reject requests according to company approval workflow
Track request processing through to completion
Monitor encashment requests in Finance menu for payroll integration
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Admin/manager has centralized visibility of all employee air ticket requests. Requests can be reviewed, approved, and tracked through completion. Approved requests integrate with payroll system for processing.
rules_and_constraints: Only HR Admin, Super Admin, or People Manager can access the employee air tickets view
Requests must follow the approval workflow configured in Automations > Approval flows
Encashment requests appear in both Air Tickets and Finance modules
edge_cases: Multiple requests from same employee may be in different stages of approval
Encashment requests require coordination with payroll processing timeline

what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: Once an air ticket request is processed or booked, users cannot delete, reverse, or change its state. This includes inability to revert rejected requests to pending, move processed requests between payroll periods, or delete test/erroneous entries after finalization. The system enforces a rigid, one-way workflow that prevents correction of submission errors or administrative adjustments.
what_to_watch_out_for.documented_workaround: For payroll-processed tickets, users must manually reopen payroll periods, delete transactions, and move them to pending status—a complex, error-prone process.

what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The platform lacks critical policy configuration capabilities: air ticket eligibility does not reset on calendar year (tracks from first request date instead), carry-over of unused balances is not supported, auto-encashment cannot be configured, employees cannot be assigned to multiple policies, and request eligibility conditions cannot be defined. These gaps prevent organizations from implementing flexible, standards-compliant air ticket benefit programs.

The platform's RBAC model restricts air ticket policy configuration exclusively to super administrators, and HR admins cannot self-service clone or create custom roles. This creates bureaucratic bottlenecks requiring development team intervention for routine administrative tasks like role duplication and policy setup.

The air ticket approval workflow requires sequential multi-step authorization (line manager before super admin) that is not immediately obvious to users. Additionally, the platform cannot notify or tag line managers when they are outside the standard approval flow, such as when proxy requesters or managers raise requests for employees.

The platform lacks a dedicated management section for air ticket requests with specialized handling for auto-encashment scenarios. Users cannot self-service view policy creation metadata and audit trail information, and the admin air ticket page lacks responsive design elements (shrinking tab bars and filter headers during scrolling).
what_to_watch_out_for.documented_workaround: Users must engage support to retrieve historical policy details.

what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The platform lacks comprehensive employee offboarding logic for air ticket policy handling and does not automatically integrate air ticket entitlements with EOS calculations. Employees who leave before using their air ticket must manually handle benefit conversion or forfeiture, creating compliance and administrative risks.

The platform lacks automated logic for dynamic air ticket allowance reimbursement calculations and cannot automatically process costs while respecting annual policy limits. Additionally, there is no granular control over ticket status transitions to prevent moving tickets to pending state based on policy or employee status.

The platform has inconsistent workflows for air ticket booking request creation with potential variability between environments. Additionally, employees lack a unified interface to manage air ticket requests under various policy conditions, particularly for encashment scenarios.

The Work Expenses module lacks robust air ticket and travel expense tracking mechanisms. The platform has incomplete workflows for submitting, processing, and approving corporate travel expenses, limiting the module's effectiveness for comprehensive expense management.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: No granular control over employee Air Ticket submission
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: HR Admins cannot disable employee-initiated Air Ticket requests while maintaining automated workflow generation. This prevents organizations from restricting expense submission to specific user groups or requiring manager pre-approval before formal requests are created.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Processed Air Ticket bookings cannot be reverted to pending status
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: Once an Air Ticket request is marked as processed (booking confirmed), users cannot revert it to pending status or make corrections. This rigid status management prevents fixing errors or adjusting travel arrangements after booking confirmation.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: No UI for Air Ticket policy configuration in company settings
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: HR Admins lacks advanced configuration options in the existing interface to manage and configure Air Ticket policies directly within company settings. This prevents granular policy customization such as approval thresholds, allowed airlines, booking windows, or cost limits.

_contradiction_resolved.original_limitation: HR Admins lack a user interface to manage and configure Air Ticket policies directly within company settings. This prevents granular policy customization such as approval thresholds, allowed airlines, booking windows, or cost limits.
_contradiction_resolved.resolution: Reworded: limitation is about advanced/granular configuration, not absence of basic UI

gaps_identified: Finance Manager: No dedicated task for processing air ticket encashments or managing payroll deductions. Finance Manager role is mentioned in integration context but lacks explicit workflow task.
People Manager has minimal issue coverage (1 issue); most approval workflow gaps affect HR Admin and Finance Manager more broadly
Finance Manager: No reported issues with Air Ticket feature (may indicate limited involvement in travel expense workflows or underreporting)

execution_order: 2. EXPLORATION - Navigate to feature, discover all UI elements using exploration_phase instructions
3. IDENTIFY ENTITY - Determine the primary item/entity using primary_entity discovery
4. CRUD TEST - Test create, read, update, delete for the primary entity
5. WORKFLOW CHECK - Verify if feature has workflow triggers/actions
6. APPROVAL FLOW - Test approval process if present
7. WHAT_TO_DO - Execute each task from what_to_do array, validate expected outcomes
8. WHAT_TO_WATCH_OUT_FOR - Validate known limitations and edge cases
9. DOCUMENTATION - Capture screenshots of every significant state change
10. REPORT - Generate validation report with pass/fail for each task and issue
